protocol.phase_c_d_skipped: proptest and derivability filter require runtime infrastructure

phase_a_results.description: Single-pass spec generation with sibling specs as context

C1_add.verdict: too_weak (missing nat identity and bounds propagation)

C2_negate.verdict: too_weak (missing intermediate formula and structural eq)

issues: says canonical_nat(r) == canonical_nat(self) but reduce is a standalone fn not method
says limbs_bounded 51 but GT says 52 (reduce outputs 52-bit limbs, not 51)
C3_reduce.verdict: wrong (incorrect bound level and self-referential spec)

C4_from_bytes.note: Uses %p() instead of GT's % pow2(255) — SEMANTICALLY DIFFERENT. from_bytes strips bit 255, not reduces mod p. This is an incorrect spec.
C4_from_bytes.verdict: wrong (% p() vs % pow2(255) is semantically different)

extra: is_uniform_bytes(&result.0) — hallucinated, compress output is deterministic not uniform

issues: Uses u8_32_as_nat(&spec_clamp_integer(bytes)) instead of scalar_as_nat(&Scalar{bytes: spec_clamp_integer(bytes)}) — different: scalar_as_nat interprets as little-endian scalar, u8_32_as_nat interprets as byte-level nat

C7_sub.note: Bounds clause is conditional but GT has unconditional. Sub always produces 52-bit output.
C7_sub.verdict: too_weak (missing structural eq and unconditional bounds)

C8_pow2k.note: Uses u64_5_as_nat instead of GT's fe51_as_nat — semantically equivalent since fe51_as_nat = u64_5_as_nat

phase_b_results.description: Bounds supplement pass on field functions

C2_negate.verdict: still too_weak (intermediate formula not a bounds property)

new_clauses_added: identity-when-already-51-bit (similar direction to GT's identity clause)
C3_reduce.verdict: still wrong (base spec was wrong, bounds can't fix it)

C4_from_bytes.fixed_from_too_weak: N/A (base spec was wrong — %p instead of %pow2(255))

phase_e_results.description: Reactive strengthening from degraded caller errors

C2_negate.evaluation: PARTIAL — proposed clause is a consequence of the GT clause but less precise. It captures the 15p/16p dichotomy but not the exact formula. Would unblock the caller's proof.

C3_reduce.evaluation: WRONG DIRECTION — fe51_canonical_limbs doesn't exist, and the proposal conflates reduce with canonical form. The GT just needs spec_reduce structural equality.

end_to_end_accuracy.after_phase_a_plus_b: 1/8 correct (Phase B didn't fully fix any too_weak case)
end_to_end_accuracy.note: The recipe's Phase A accuracy (1/8) is LOWER than Exp 9's single-pass (4/10 = 40%). This is likely because: (1) 18d prompts used body SUMMARIES instead of full bodies, (2) the model was sonnet not opus, (3) some specs (C3 reduce, C4 from_bytes) got fundamentally wrong answers rather than just missing clauses.

comparison.note: 18d underperforms prior experiments. Key difference: 18d used body SUMMARIES (not full function bodies) to keep prompts compact. This lost critical information — e.g., C4 from_bytes does '%pow2(255)' masking which only appears in the actual code, not the summary. The recipe should use FULL function bodies.

key_findings.finding_1: Body summaries are INSUFFICIENT for spec generation. The model needs the actual code to infer the right spec. C3 (reduce) and C4 (from_bytes) both got fundamentally wrong specs because the summary omitted key implementation details.
key_findings.finding_2: Phase B (bounds supplement) adds true-but-not-GT clauses and partially recovers missing bounds (C1 got 51->52). But it can't fix fundamentally wrong Phase A specs.
key_findings.finding_3: Phase E (reactive strengthening) works when the error message directly names the missing property (C2: partial fix). Fails when the error is about structural equality (C3: wrong direction).
key_findings.finding_4: C8 (pow2k) was the only correct spec — it has the simplest relationship (repeated squaring) and its sibling specs (square, square2) directly model the pattern.
key_findings.finding_5: Sibling specs help when the target function is structurally similar to siblings. C5 (compress) and C6 (mul_base_clamped) had less-similar siblings and got wrong specs.

pipeline_recommendation.critical: Use FULL function bodies in spec generation prompts, not summaries
pipeline_recommendation.phase_a: Effective for algebraic properties when full body is provided (Exp 9 showed 100% algebraic accuracy with full bodies)
pipeline_recommendation.phase_b: Marginal value — adds some bounds clauses but doesn't fix fundamental errors
pipeline_recommendation.phase_e: Useful for the ~30% of too-weak specs where the caller error names the missing property
pipeline_recommendation.key_limitation: Implementation-level intermediate formulas (negate carry, reduce exact nat) remain systematically missed across all approaches
